news_detail_lead: A community event connecting culture, education, sustainability, and local participation in Okayama.
news_detail_p1: The Community Learning Festival brings together local residents, students, volunteers, educators, and international participants to learn from each other and strengthen community connections.
news_detail_p2: Through workshops, presentations, cultural activities, and discussion sessions, participants can experience how UNESCO activities support peace, education, culture, and sustainability.
news_detail_highlights_text: The program includes community learning workshops, local culture activities, volunteer introductions, and opportunities to discuss sustainable development goals.
news_detail_participation_text: Anyone interested in education, culture, sustainability, or local community activities is welcome to participate. More details will be announced through the activities page.
page_title_news_detail: Community Learning Festival 2026 | Okayama UNESCO Association
place_item2_text: Historical streets, traditions, and local learning.
greeting_subtitle: A message about peace, education, community action, and the future direction of Okayama UNESCO Association.
greeting_section_title: Each person’s heart is the starting point of peace
greeting_p1: In 2026, the world faces overlapping crises, including wars, conflicts, and growing uncertainty. In such a time, we need to keep a strong foundation of peace in our hearts and remember that a peaceful society begins with our own choices and actions.
greeting_p2: Peace is not something we simply wait to receive. It begins inside each person and is shaped through daily actions. Even small acts of kindness, tolerance, and dialogue in our homes, workplaces, and communities can gradually change society.
greeting_p3: This year marks the 75th anniversary of Japan joining UNESCO. To open a path toward a better future, we value three actions: raising our voices for peace, passing the roots of peace to the next generation through education, and expanding grassroots connections with people around the world.
greeting_p4: Education is one of the strongest foundations for sustainable peace. By helping children and young people develop empathy, respect for diversity, and the ability to solve problems through dialogue, we can build a future based not on hatred, but on understanding.
greeting_p5: Okayama UNESCO Association will continue activities such as SDGs Passport presentations, children’s art exhibitions, Peace Bell events, the Okayama UNESCO Festival, and multicultural study cafés. Through these activities, we hope to share the light of peace in each person’s heart and expand multicultural coexistence in our community.
greeting_signature:
June 16, 2026
Okayama UNESCO Association President
Junko Tokuyama
